the ® isoform in melanocytes [49], and further showed that PKC inhibitor reduces pigmentation in murine skin in vivo [50]. It is interesting to assume that the B isoform is indispensable for the regulation of the tyrosinase activity, but melanin is detected even in the melanoma cells lacking the PKC ® isoform [25,27]. On the other hand, the a isoform is increased in murine B16 melanoma cells treated with retinoic acid [37,39], and the induction as well as the overexpression of this

PKC isoform enhances the melanin synthesis, suggesting the involvement of the a isoform in melanogenesis [38]. The level of the a isoform is also elevated in B16 cells cultured with mannosylerythiritol lipid, a novel extracellular glycolipid from yeast that induces differentiation of HL-60 promyelocytic leukemia cells [44].
